title: Fibonacci — fix memoization bug via targeted edit
prompt:
The file fib.py has a memoization bug in `fib_memo`: the function stores computed results in `memo` but never checks `memo` BEFORE recomputing. This makes it asymptotically no faster than the naive recursive version.

Edit the function to add a memo-lookup check at the start of `fib_memo`. The fix is to insert one line: `if n in memo: return memo[n]` immediately after `memo = {}` line is handled (i.e., before the `if n < 2` line).

Use the edit tool's insert operation with a precise hash anchor. Do NOT rewrite the function. Then report the single inserted line.
expected_rubric: Insert (not full-file rewrite) applied. Hash-anchored edit insert count >= 1. No 'string not found' failures on hash-anchored path. Final file contains 'if n in memo: return memo[n]'.
notes: D-10 edit-heavy augmentation of CODE_02. Forces insert-after-hash-anchor usage — the exact Hashline capability that string-replace often struggles with when there are multiple similar-looking lines.
